diagrams.title: Full Adder Circuit
diagrams.description: A full adder is a combinational circuit that adds three binary inputs (A, B, and Cin) and produces two outputs (Sum and Cout). This diagram shows the gate-level implementation using XOR, AND, and OR gates, which is fundamental to understanding how arithmetic operations are performed in computer processors.
elements: Second AND gate (Cin AND partial sum)
diagrams.purpose: Explains how binary addition works at the circuit level, demonstrating the relationship between logical operations and arithmetic. Essential for understanding ALU design and ripple carry adders.

diagrams.title: 2-to-4 Line Decoder
diagrams.description: A 2-to-4 decoder has 2 input lines and 4 output lines. Based on the binary value of the 2 inputs, exactly one of the 4 outputs is activated (set to logic HIGH). This diagram shows how binary information is decoded to select one specific line out of multiple possibilities.
elements: Output connection lines with active state highlighted
diagrams.purpose: Demonstrates the decoding concept where n inputs select one of 2^n outputs. This is fundamental to memory addressing, selection circuits, and is the basis for demultiplexers. Helps students understand how binary addresses select specific memory locations.

diagrams.title: 4-to-1 Multiplexer
diagrams.description: A 4-to-1 multiplexer (MUX) has 4 data input lines, 2 select lines, and 1 output. The select lines determine which of the 4 input signals is forwarded to the single output. This diagram illustrates the selection mechanism essential for data routing in computer systems.
elements: 4 AND gates (each input ANDed with decoded select)
diagrams.purpose: Explains how multiplexers enable multiple data sources to share a single transmission path. Critical for understanding data buses, switch logic, and how processors select between different memory or I/O devices. Forms the basis for data routing in CPUs and communication systems.
